questions.question: Explain the geographical location of India.
questions.answer: India is located in South Asia and lies entirely in the Northern Hemisphere. The mainland extends between latitudes 8°4'N and 37°6'N and longitudes 68°7'E and 97°25'E.

questions.question: Explain the significance of the Tropic of Cancer in India.
questions.answer: The Tropic of Cancer passes almost through the middle of India. It divides the country into tropical and subtropical regions and influences climate patterns.
answer_outline: Divides India into two climatic zones

questions.question: Explain the size of India and its rank in the world.
questions.answer: India covers about 3.28 million square kilometres and is the seventh largest country in the world.

questions.question: Explain the physical boundaries of India.
questions.answer: India is bounded by the Himalayas in the north and surrounded by the Arabian Sea in the west, the Bay of Bengal in the east and the Indian Ocean in the south.

questions.question: Explain why India uses Indian Standard Time.
questions.answer: India uses Indian Standard Time based on the longitude 82°30'E to maintain uniform time across the country.

questions.question: Explain the importance of India's central location.
questions.answer: India's central location between East and West Asia helped establish trade and cultural contacts with many regions.

questions.question: Explain how India's location helped cultural exchange.
questions.answer: India's location along major trade routes allowed exchange of ideas, goods and culture with other civilizations.

questions.question: Explain the role of land routes in India's contacts with the world.
questions.answer: Mountain passes across the Himalayas allowed travellers and traders to reach India through land routes.

questions.question: Explain the role of oceans in India's trade.
questions.answer: Oceans provided routes for maritime trade connecting India with many countries.

questions.question: Explain the significance of the Indian Ocean for India.
questions.answer: The Indian Ocean connects India with important international sea routes and trade networks.

questions.question: Explain why India is called a subcontinent.
questions.answer: India is called a subcontinent because of its large size, geographical diversity and distinct identity.

questions.question: Explain how the opening of the Suez Canal helped India.
questions.answer: The opening of the Suez Canal reduced the distance between India and Europe by about 7,000 km.

questions.question: Explain the importance of India's neighbouring countries.
questions.answer: Neighbouring countries influence India's trade, cultural exchanges and geopolitical relations.

questions.question: Explain the land neighbours of India.
questions.answer: India shares land borders with Pakistan, Afghanistan, China, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh and Myanmar.

questions.question: Explain the island neighbours of India.
questions.answer: Sri Lanka and Maldives are island neighbours of India located across the sea.

questions.question: Explain how Sri Lanka is separated from India.
questions.answer: Sri Lanka is separated from India by the Palk Strait and the Gulf of Mannar.

questions.question: Explain the location of the Maldives Islands.
questions.answer: The Maldives Islands are located to the south of the Lakshadweep Islands in the Arabian Sea.

questions.question: Explain the island groups of India.
questions.answer: India has two main island groups: Lakshadweep in the Arabian Sea and Andaman and Nicobar Islands in the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Explain why India has a long coastline.
questions.answer: India has a long coastline because the peninsular region extends into the Indian Ocean.

questions.question: Explain the importance of sea routes for India.
questions.answer: Sea routes connect India with many countries and promote international trade.

questions.question: Explain how trade routes influenced India's history.
questions.answer: Trade routes allowed movement of goods, people and ideas between India and other regions.

questions.question: Explain why India has diverse cultural influences.
questions.answer: India's contacts with many civilizations through trade led to diverse cultural influences.

questions.question: Explain the importance of India's geographical diversity.
questions.answer: India's large geographical area results in diverse landforms, climates and cultures.

questions.question: Explain why India has strategic importance in South Asia.
questions.answer: India's central location and connections with neighbouring countries make it strategically important.

questions.question: Explain the overall significance of India's location.
questions.answer: India's location supports trade, cultural exchange, communication and strategic influence globally.
